insights.content: Route to search when the query depends on post-cutoff or time-sensitive facts (e.g., “latest”, “today”, years after 2024, releases, prices, schedules, rankings).
Use search for specific, externally-verifiable facts about named entities (stats, dates, versions, locations, company facts) rather than relying on internal recall.
Perform a cheap probe: run at most one initial search when external knowledge is plausible but uncertain, given the low cost and moderate success rate.
Limit search to at most two iterations; if results remain inconclusive or conflicting, stop searching.
Disambiguate proper nouns via search when the query could reference multiple entities (e.g., organizations or products with similar names).
Avoid sending sensitive, private, or proprietary content to search; redact or summarize before querying, or stay in answer mode if redaction harms utility.
Skip search for self-contained reasoning, math, or explanatory requests that don’t require external data; answer directly.
Switch to answer mode once a single authoritative source or two independent sources corroborate the needed fact; synthesize and cite.
If two search attempts fail to produce credible, consistent results, ask a clarifying question or provide an uncertainty-qualified answer instead of continuing to search.
When the user explicitly requests citations or sources, use search to gather them, then move to answer to present the response with references.
Stop searching once all required facts for the user’s intent are gathered; compose the final answer rather than accumulating more sources.
If a query primarily requires multi-step calculation, simulation, or parsing/transformation, use code mode instead of search.

time_locale.geolocated_release_dates.name: Geolocated Release Dates
time_locale.geolocated_release_dates.description: Return release dates specific to a country/region; disambiguate medium (film vs book) before answering.
indicators: when does/when did X come out
examples: when does a wrinkle in time come out in canada?
when did end of the road come out?

disambiguation.media_work_sense.name: Media/Work Sense Disambiguation
disambiguation.media_work_sense.description: Resolve whether a title refers to a book, film, song, album, or different regional version before retrieving facts.
examples: when did end of the road come out?
how many episodes of touching evil are there?
when does a wrinkle in time come out in canada?

entity.gov_creation_and_leadership.name: Government Agency Creation and Leadership
entity.gov_creation_and_leadership.description: Recall which president supported/created agencies and who was appointed to lead them.
examples: which president supported the creation of the environmental protection agency (epa)?
who was appointed to manage the national recovery administration?

pragmatics.answer_instead_of_deflecting.name: Answer vs. Deflect
pragmatics.answer_instead_of_deflecting.description: Avoid generic 'I don't have real-time data' or requests for more info when a reasonable, static or canonical answer exists.
indicators: I don't have access to real-time information
examples: when does a wrinkle in time come out in canada?
who won big brother head of household canada?

pragmatics.assumption_with_disclosure.name: State Assumptions Explicitly
pragmatics.assumption_with_disclosure.description: When disambiguating, state the assumed interpretation (e.g., the 2018 film vs the book) before answering.
indicators: I believe you are referring to
examples: when does a wrinkle in time come out in canada?
who talks for belle in beauty and the beast?

pragmatics.units_and_currency_precision.name: Units and Currency Precision
pragmatics.units_and_currency_precision.description: Report the correct unit/currency and basis (per hour, €) when giving numeric answers.
examples: what is the minimum wage in france per hour?

disambiguation.context.scoped_fact_resol.name: Context-Scoped Fact/Count Resolution (Version/Locale & Time/Edition)
disambiguation.context.scoped_fact_resol.description: Infer the intended contextual scope—version/locale (e.g., original vs remake; UK vs US) and/or time slice (year, date, era, season, week, episode, tournament/edition)—from the query and return facts, outcomes, participation details, or counts specific to that slice. Avoid conflating across versions or aggregating across time; seek brief clarification only when the scope remains ambiguous.
indicators: Media series with multiple versions/remakes or regional editions; requires version/locale-specific episode counts.
Queries scoped by year/date/era/season/week/episode for shows, sports, or events.
Edition-specific event participation/outcomes (e.g., particular Olympic Games, World Cup editions).
Requests where defaulting to latest or cumulative totals would be incorrect; must avoid aggregation across scopes.
Context provides implicit scope cues (region, network, time period) that must be applied before answering.
If scope remains unclear after context inference, ask a brief clarifying question rather than assume.

entity.resolution.canonical_attribution_.name: Entity Resolution & Canonicalized Founder/Role Attribution
entity.resolution.canonical_attribution_.description: Resolve informal or ambiguous entity mentions (especially organizations/companies) to their canonical forms and return accurate, standardized founder(s) or other requested role/credit attributes. Enumerate all applicable entities with contextual qualifiers (e.g., year, version, predecessor/successor, adaptation/medium) when attribution spans multiple valid entities, avoid spurious names, and request disambiguation when needed.
indicators: User asks: Who founded <org>? / founders of <company>
Disambiguate similarly named organizations before answering
Return standardized, formal names for both entities and people
Handle multiple co-founders and list all without adding extras
Detect multi-attribution across versions/mergers/rebrandings and enumerate with qualifiers (year, version, predecessor/successor, medium)
Ask for clarification when multiple plausible entities or versions exist
